perturbation.original_text: Section 11.03. Governing Law; Jurisdiction. Any disputes relating to, arising out of or resulting from this Agreement, including to its execution, performance, or enforcement, shall be governed by, and construed in accordance with, the Laws of the State of Delaware, regardless of the Laws that might otherwise govern under applicable principles of conflicts of Laws thereof. Each Party irrevocably consents to the exclusive jurisdiction, forum and venue of the Delaware Court of Chancery (and if the Delaware Court of Chancery shall be unavailable, any Delaware State court or the federal court sitting in the State of Delaware) over any and all claims, disputes, controversies or disagreements between the Parties or any of their respective Affiliates, successors and assigns under or related to this Agreement or any of the transactions contemplated hereby, including their execution, performance or enforcement, whether in contract, tort or otherwise. Each of the Parties hereby agrees that it shall not assert, and shall hereby waive, any claim or right or defense that it is not subject to the jurisdiction of such courts, that the venue is improper, that the forum is inconvenient or any similar objection, claim or argument. Each Party agrees that a final judgment in any legal proceeding resolved in accordance with this Section 11.03, Section 11.04, Section 11.05 and Section 11.06 shall be conclusive and may be enforced in other jurisdictions by suit on the judgment or in any other manner provided by applicable Law.
perturbation.changed_text: Section 11.03. Governing Law; Jurisdiction. Any disputes relating to, arising out of or resulting from this Agreement, including to its execution, performance, or enforcement, shall be governed by, and construed in accordance with, the Laws of the State of Delaware, regardless of the Laws that might otherwise govern under applicable principles of conflicts of Laws thereof. Each Party consents to the jurisdiction, forum and venue of the Delaware Court of Chancery (and if the Delaware Court of Chancery shall be unavailable, any Delaware State court or the federal court sitting in the State of Delaware) over any and all claims, disputes, controversies or disagreements between the Parties or any of their respective Affiliates, successors and assigns under or related to this Agreement or any of the transactions contemplated hereby, including their execution, performance or enforcement, whether in contract, tort or otherwise. Each Party agrees that a final judgment in any legal proceeding resolved in accordance with this Section 11.03, Section 11.04, Section 11.05 and Section 11.06 shall be conclusive and may be enforced in other jurisdictions by suit on the judgment or in any other manner provided by applicable Law.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the word "irrevocably" and the waiver of rights to object to jurisdiction and venue, this change allows a party to later challenge the jurisdiction and venue, potentially undermining the enforceability of the contract. This creates uncertainty and could increase legal costs.
perturbation.law_explanation: The original clause contains language where Each of the Parties hereby agrees that it shall not assert, and shall hereby waive, any claim or right or defense that it is not subject to the jurisdiction of such courts, that the venue is improper, that the forum is inconvenient or any similar objection, claim or argument. The removal of this clause gives a party the ability to assert a change of venue or jurisdiction under 28 U.S. Code § 1404, making the previously agreed upon Delaware jurisdiction no longer guaranteed.

perturbation.scraped_snippet_2: 28 U.S. Code § 1404 - Change of venue | U.S. Code | US Law | LII / Legal Information Institute Please help us improve our site! × No thank you Quick search by citation: Title Section Go! 28 U.S. Code § 1404 - Change of venue U.S. Code Notes prev | next (a) For the convenience of parties and witnesses, in the interest of justice, a district court may transfer any civil action to any other district or division where it might have been brought or to any district or division to which all parties have consented. (b) Upon motion, consent or stipulation of all parties, any action, suit or proceeding of a civil nature or any motion or hearing thereof, may be transferred, in the discretion of the court, from the division in which pending to any other division in the same district . Transfer of proceedings in rem brought by or on behalf of the United States may be transferred under this section without the consent of the United States where all other parties request transfer. (c) A district court may order any civil action to be tried at any place within the division in which it is pending. (d) Transfers from a district court of the United States to the District Court of Guam, the District Court for the Northern Mariana Islands, or the District Court of the Virgin Islands shall not be permitted under this section. As otherwise used in this section, the term “ district court ” includes the District Court of Guam, the District Court for the Northern Mariana Islands, and the District Court of the Virgin Islands, and the term “district” includes the territorial jurisdiction of each such court. (June 25, 1948, ch. 646, 62 Stat. 937 ; Pub. L. 87–845, § 9 , Oct. 18, 1962 , 76A Stat. 699 ; Pub. L. 104–317, title VI, § 610(a) , Oct. 19, 1996 , 110 Stat. 3860 ; Pub. L. 112–63, title II, § 204 , Dec. 7, 2011 , 125 Stat. 764 .) perturbation.justification: The original contract text includes an explicit waiver of the right to object to jurisdiction and venue. By removing the word 'irrevocably' and the waiver clause, the changed text contradicts the original agreement. The law explanation and 28 U.S. Code § 1404, as shown in both scraped snippets, supports the ability to request a change of venue. Therefore, the removal of the waiver allows a party to potentially undermine the enforceability of the contract, which constitutes a legal contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Section 11.04. Waiver of Jury Trial. EACH PARTY HEREBY WAIVES ITS RIGHTS TO A JURY TRIAL OF ANY CLAIM OR CAUSE OF ACTION RELATING TO, ARISING OUT OF OR RESULTING FROM THIS AGREEMENT OR THE TRANSACTIONS CONTEMPLATED HEREBY, INCLUDING THEIR EXECUTION, PERFORMANCE OR ENFORCEMENT, WHETHER IN CONTRACT, TORT OR OTHERWISE. THE SCOPE OF THIS WAIVER IS INTENDED TO BE ALL-ENCOMPASSING OF ANY AND ALL DISPUTES THAT MAY BE FILED IN ANY COURT AND THAT RELATE TO THE SUBJECT MATTER OF THIS TRANSACTION, INCLUDING CONTRACT CLAIMS, TORT CLAIMS (INCLUDING NEGLIGENCE), BREACH OF DUTY CLAIMS AND ALL OTHER COMMON LAW AND STATUTORY CLAIMS. THIS SECTION HAS BEEN FULLY DISCUSSED BY EACH OF THE PARTIES AND THESE PROVISIONS WILL NOT BE SUBJECT TO ANY EXCEPTIONS.
perturbation.changed_text: Section 11.04. Waiver of Jury Trial. EACH PARTY HEREBY WAIVES ITS RIGHTS TO A JURY TRIAL OF ANY CLAIM OR CAUSE OF ACTION RELATING TO, ARISING OUT OF OR RESULTING FROM THIS AGREEMENT OR THE TRANSACTIONS CONTEMPLATED HEREBY, INCLUDING THEIR EXECUTION, PERFORMANCE OR ENFORCEMENT, WHETHER IN CONTRACT, TORT OR OTHERWISE. THE SCOPE OF THIS WAIVER IS INTENDED TO BE ALL-ENCOMPASSING OF ANY AND ALL DISPUTES THAT MAY BE FILED IN ANY COURT AND THAT RELATE TO THE SUBJECT MATTER OF THIS TRANSACTION, INCLUDING CONTRACT CLAIMS, TORT CLAIMS (INCLUDING NEGLIGENCE), BREACH OF DUTY CLAIMS AND ALL OTHER COMMON LAW AND STATUTORY CLAIMS.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the final sentence ("THIS SECTION HAS BEEN FULLY DISCUSSED BY EACH OF THE PARTIES AND THESE PROVISIONS WILL NOT BE SUBJECT TO ANY EXCEPTIONS."), it weakens the enforceability of the jury trial waiver. This could be challenged, arguing that the waiver was not knowingly and voluntarily made, thus potentially enabling a jury trial where one was intended to be waived.
perturbation.law_explanation: The Seventh Amendment guarantees the right to a jury trial in civil cases. The deletion of the final sentence weakens the argument that the waiver was knowing and voluntary, as is needed to waive a constitutional right. By removing the language confirming full discussion and no exceptions, a party could argue the waiver was not properly understood or agreed upon, thus conflicting with the protections afforded by the Seventh Amendment.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_2: Seventh Amendment | U.S. Constitution | US Law | LII / Legal Information Institute Please help us improve our site! × No thank you Seventh Amendment The Seventh Amendment continues a practice from English common law of distinguishing civil claims which must be tried before a jury (absent waiver by the parties) from claims and issues that may be heard by a judge alone. It only governs federal civil courts and has no application to civil courts set up by the states when those courts are hearing only disputes of state law . Learn more... Primary tabs Amendment VII In suits at common law, where the value in controversy shall exceed twenty dollars, the right of trial by jury shall be preserved, and no fact tried by a jury, shall be otherwise reexamined in any court of the United States, than according to the rules of the common law. Wex Resources Criminal Law Criminal Procedure Jury Other Resources US Code: Title 18 Federal Rules of Criminal Procedure ‹ Sixth Amendment up Eighth Amendment › U.S. Constitution Toolbox Explanation of the Constitution - from the Congressional Research Service
perturbation.justification: The removal of the sentence 'THIS SECTION HAS BEEN FULLY DISCUSSED BY EACH OF THE PARTIES AND THESE PROVISIONS WILL NOT BE SUBJECT TO ANY EXCEPTIONS' weakens the enforceability of the jury trial waiver. The Seventh Amendment, as noted in Scraped Law Snippet 2, preserves the right to trial by jury. To waive this right, the waiver must be knowing and voluntary. The deleted sentence provided evidence of this knowing and voluntary agreement. Its removal makes it easier to argue the waiver was not properly understood or agreed upon. This creates a legal loophole that could render the waiver unenforceable, thus contradicting the intent and protection offered by the Seventh Amendment.

perturbation.original_text: Section 11.11. Severability. If any provision of this Agreement or the application thereof to any Person or circumstance is determined by a court of competent jurisdiction to be invalid, void or unenforceable, the remaining provisions hereof, or the application of such provision to Persons or circumstances, or in jurisdictions other than those as to which it has been held invalid or unenforceable, shall remain in full force and effect and shall in no way be affected, impaired or invalidated thereby, so long as the economic or legal substance of the transactions contemplated hereby is not affected in any manner materially adverse to either Party. Upon any such determination, any such provision, to the extent determined to be invalid, void or unenforceable, shall be deemed replaced by a provision that such court determines is valid and enforceable and that comes closest to expressing the intention of the invalid, void or unenforceable provision.
perturbation.changed_text: Section 11.11. Severability. If any provision of this Agreement or the application thereof to any Person or circumstance is determined by a court of competent jurisdiction to be invalid, void or unenforceable, the remaining provisions hereof, or the application of such provision to Persons or circumstances, or in jurisdictions other than those as to which it has been held invalid or unenforceable, shall remain in full force and effect and shall in no way be affected, impaired or invalidated thereby, so long as the economic or legal substance of the transactions contemplated hereby is not affected in any manner materially adverse to either Party.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the second sentence ("Upon any such determination, any such provision, to the extent determined to be invalid, void or unenforceable, shall be deemed replaced by a provision that such court determines is valid and enforceable and that comes closest to expressing the intention of the invalid, void or unenforceable provision."), the contract lacks a mechanism for replacing an invalid clause with one that aligns with the original intent. This omission contradicts the principle of contract reformation, where courts attempt to uphold the contract's intent, and could lead to the entire agreement being deemed unenforceable if a critical clause is struck down.
perturbation.law_explanation: The Restatement (Second) of Contracts § 158 discusses reformation as a remedy for mistake. While not a direct statute, it reflects the common law principle that courts may reform a contract to reflect the parties' original intent. The absence of a clause allowing for replacement of invalid provisions hinders the court's ability to reform the contract and uphold its overall purpose, thereby potentially contradicting this common law principle.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_2: reformation | Wex | US Law | LII / Legal Information Institute Please help us improve our site! × No thank you reformation Reformation is a court’s equitable power to modify a contract to reflect the parties’ true intent where some error has been committed. Courts require plaintiffs seeking to reform a contract to prove certain elements, however. The Oregon Supreme Court in Jensen v. Miller summarizes the common law requirements for showing reformation, stating that parties seeking reformation must show: “(1) that there was an antecedent agreement to which the contract can be reformed; (2) that there was a mutual mistake or a unilateral mistake on the part of the party seeking reformation and inequitable conduct on the part of the other party; and (3) that the party seeking reformation was not guilty of gross negligence .” For example, in Central Oregon Independent Health Services, Inc. v. State , an Oregon Court of Appeals held that a plaintiff stated a claim for reformation by stating that previous contracts required parties to “pay at the correctly calculated rates,” as opposed to the current contract which had been construed “to impose no obligation on [the parties] to correct erroneously calculated rates.” [Last reviewed in December of 2020 by the Wex Definitions Team ] Wex COMMERCE THE LEGAL PROCESS contracts wex definitions Wex Toolbox
perturbation.justification: The modified contract removes a crucial element of the severability clause: the mechanism for replacing invalid provisions with ones that reflect the original intent. This omission directly undermines the principle of contract reformation, as described in the Law Explanation, where courts aim to uphold the contract's purpose. Scraped Law Snippet 2 defines reformation as 'a court’s equitable power to modify a contract to reflect the parties’ true intent where some error has been committed.' By deleting the clause that allows for such replacement, the contract actively hinders the court's ability to enact reformation, thus creating a legal contradiction.
